# tokens: # A list or map appearing inside `${...}` is a normal sub-engine value.
# At the sub-engine's end-of-Run, autoEvalStack collapses eval-flagged
# lists by running their contents as a sub-program, so an expression
# list with words inside (`[1 addq 2]`) becomes its result before
# stringification. Map values auto-evaluate too (paren-valued entries,
# embedded eval-lists, def references in value position).
# Stringification falls through ValToString's default branch to
# Value.String(), which formats lists as `[a,b,c]` and maps as
# `{k:v,k:v}` — comma-separated, no spaces, map keys in sorted order.
